module.title: ADVANCED JOINS
module.subtitle: LEFT JOIN · multi-table · self-join · join+aggregate
module.theory:
## The grid you are jacking into
Three tables wired together:
~~~text
crews(id, name, sector)
runners(id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id)
contracts(id, runner_id, target, payout)
~~~
A **runner** belongs to a **crew** (crew_id) and may have a **mentor** who is *another runner* (mentor_id). A **contract** is a job pulled by one runner (runner_id).

## LEFT JOIN — keep every left-side row
A plain `JOIN` (inner join) only returns rows that match on **both** sides. If a runner has no crew, an inner join silently drops that runner.

A **LEFT JOIN** keeps **every** row from the **left** table (the one before `LEFT JOIN`). When the right side has no match, its columns come back as **NULL** instead of vanishing.
~~~sql
SELECT runners.alias, crews.name
FROM runners
LEFT JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id;
~~~
**WHY:** you want a full roster — including the freelancer with no crew — not just the ones who happen to match.

> INTEL — To find the *unmatched* rows (the freelancer, the empty crew), LEFT JOIN and then filter `WHERE rightTable.id IS NULL`. Those NULLs are the rows that had no partner.

> WARNING — **Left vs right matters.** `runners LEFT JOIN crews` keeps all runners; `crews LEFT JOIN runners` keeps all crews. They answer different questions. Pick the table you must not lose, and put it on the left.

## Joining three tables
Chain `JOIN` clauses. Each `ON` connects the new table to one already in the query. To walk from a contract out to the crew, hop contract → runner → crew:
~~~sql
SELECT contracts.target, runners.alias, crews.name
FROM contracts
JOIN runners ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id
JOIN crews   ON runners.crew_id = crews.id;
~~~
**WHY:** the fact you need (the target) lives in one table but the labels (alias, crew name) live in others. Joins reassemble the full picture.

## Self-join — one table, two roles
When a table points back at itself (a runner's `mentor_id` is another runner's `id`), join the table **to itself** using two **aliases** so SQLite can tell the two copies apart:
~~~sql
SELECT r.alias AS student, m.alias AS mentor
FROM runners AS r
JOIN runners AS m ON r.mentor_id = m.id;
~~~
Here `r` is the student copy and `m` is the mentor copy of the *same* table.

> WARNING — Without aliases, `SELECT alias FROM runners JOIN runners ...` is **ambiguous** — SQLite cannot tell which copy you mean and the query errors. The aliases (`r`, `m`) are mandatory.

## JOIN + GROUP BY + an aggregate
Join first to stitch the rows together, then `GROUP BY` to fold them into buckets and run an aggregate (`COUNT`, `SUM`, `AVG`...). Counting **contracts per runner** with a LEFT JOIN keeps runners who pulled **zero** jobs (their count is 0):
~~~sql
SELECT runners.alias, COUNT(contracts.id) AS jobs
FROM runners
LEFT JOIN contracts ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id
GROUP BY runners.id;
~~~
> INTEL — Count the column from the **right** table (`COUNT(contracts.id)`), not `COUNT(*)`. `COUNT(*)` counts rows — and a LEFT-JOINed runner with no contract still has one NULL row, so `COUNT(*)` would wrongly report 1. `COUNT(contracts.id)` ignores NULLs and correctly reports 0.

exercises.title: FULL ROSTER
exercises.prompt: List **every** runner with the **name** of their crew. Return two columns: runners.**alias** and crews.**name**. Runners with no crew must still appear (their crew name will be NULL). Use a LEFT JOIN. Order by runners.alias (A→Z).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT runners.alias, crews.name FROM runners JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id ORDER BY runners.alias;
exercises.solution: SELECT runners.alias, crews.name FROM runners LEFT JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id ORDER BY runners.alias;
exercises.hint: LEFT JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id — the LEFT keeps the crewless runner.
exercises.lore: Even the unaffiliated leave footprints on the grid.

exercises.title: FREELANCER
exercises.prompt: Find the runner(s) who belong to **no crew**. LEFT JOIN runners to crews, then keep only the rows where the crew did not match (crews.id IS NULL). Return one column: runners.**alias**. Order does not matter.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.solution: SELECT runners.alias FROM runners LEFT JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id WHERE crews.id IS NULL;
exercises.hint: After the LEFT JOIN, the unmatched rows are exactly the ones WHERE crews.id IS NULL.
exercises.lore: No colours, no banner — only a handle and a price.

exercises.title: EMPTY CREW
exercises.brief: A crew with nobody in it.
exercises.prompt: Find the crew(s) that have **no runners** assigned. LEFT JOIN crews to runners (crews on the left this time), then keep rows where no runner matched (runners.id IS NULL). Return one column: crews.**name**. Order does not matter.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT crews.name FROM crews LEFT JOIN runners ON runners.crew_id = crews.id;
exercises.solution: SELECT crews.name FROM crews LEFT JOIN runners ON runners.crew_id = crews.id WHERE runners.id IS NULL;
exercises.hint: Put crews on the left, LEFT JOIN runners, then filter WHERE runners.id IS NULL.
exercises.lore: A banner with no soldiers behind it.

exercises.title: CONTRACT TRACE
exercises.brief: Walk contract to runner to crew.
exercises.prompt: For every contract, return three columns: contracts.**target**, runners.**alias** (who pulled it) and crews.**name** (their crew). Join all three tables (contracts → runners → crews). Order by contracts.payout **descending** (highest payout first).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT contracts.target, runners.alias FROM contracts JOIN runners ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id;
exercises.solution: SELECT contracts.target, runners.alias, crews.name FROM contracts JOIN runners ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id JOIN crews ON runners.crew_id = crews.id ORDER BY contracts.payout DESC;
exercises.hint: Two JOINs: contracts JOIN runners ON runner_id=id, then JOIN crews ON crew_id=id. Then ORDER BY contracts.payout DESC.
exercises.lore: Follow the money back to the banner that flies it.

exercises.title: MENTOR LINK
exercises.brief: Pair each student with their mentor.
exercises.prompt: Each runner's mentor_id points to another runner's id. Self-join the **runners** table to itself (aliases **r** for the student, **m** for the mentor) to pair each student with their mentor. Return two columns: the student's alias and the mentor's alias. Only runners who actually have a mentor should appear. Order by the student's alias (A→Z).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT alias, mentor_id FROM runners WHERE mentor_id IS NOT NULL;
exercises.solution: SELECT r.alias, m.alias FROM runners AS r JOIN runners AS m ON r.mentor_id = m.id ORDER BY r.alias;
exercises.hint: FROM runners AS r JOIN runners AS m ON r.mentor_id = m.id — two aliases for the same table.
exercises.lore: Every ghost in the wire learned from another ghost.

exercises.title: JOBS PER RUNNER
exercises.prompt: Count how many contracts each runner has pulled — including runners who have pulled **zero** (their count must be 0). LEFT JOIN runners to contracts, GROUP BY the runner, and COUNT the contracts. Return two columns: runners.**alias** and the count. Order by runners.alias (A→Z).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT runners.alias, COUNT(contracts.id) FROM runners JOIN contracts ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id GROUP BY runners.id ORDER BY runners.alias;
exercises.solution: SELECT runners.alias, COUNT(contracts.id) FROM runners LEFT JOIN contracts ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id GROUP BY runners.id ORDER BY runners.alias;
exercises.hint: LEFT JOIN keeps the zero-contract runners; COUNT(contracts.id) (not COUNT(*)) reports 0 for them.
exercises.lore: Some runners are all talk and no breach.

exercises.title: CREW HAUL
exercises.brief: Total payout banked by each crew.
exercises.prompt: Total the payout banked by each crew. Join crews → runners → contracts, GROUP BY the crew, and SUM contracts.payout. Return two columns: crews.**name** and the total payout. Only crews that have at least one contract will appear. Order by the total payout **descending** (biggest haul first).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT crews.name, SUM(contracts.payout) FROM crews JOIN runners ON runners.crew_id = crews.id GROUP BY crews.id;
exercises.solution: SELECT crews.name, SUM(contracts.payout) FROM crews JOIN runners ON runners.crew_id = crews.id JOIN contracts ON contracts.runner_id = runners.id GROUP BY crews.id ORDER BY SUM(contracts.payout) DESC;
exercises.hint: Three tables joined, GROUP BY crews.id, SUM(contracts.payout), then ORDER BY that sum DESC.
exercises.lore: Whichever banner banks the most eddies runs the sector.

exercises.title: MENTOR & CREW
exercises.brief: Student, mentor, and the student's crew.
exercises.prompt: For each runner who has a mentor, return three columns: the student's alias, the mentor's alias, and the **name** of the **student's** crew. Self-join runners to itself (r = student, m = mentor) and also JOIN crews on the student's crew_id. Order by the student's alias (A→Z).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE crews (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sector TEXT);
INSERT INTO crews (id, name, sector) VALUES
 (1, 'Neon Wraiths', 'Shibuya'),
 (2, 'Black ICE', 'Chiba'),
 (3, 'Ghost Lattice', 'Night City'),
 (4, 'Pale Choir', 'Watson');
CREATE TABLE runners (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, alias TEXT, crew_id INTEGER, mentor_id INTEGER);
INSERT INTO runners (id, alias, crew_id, mentor_id) VALUES
 (1, 'Cobra', 1, NULL),
 (2, 'Wisp', 1, 1),
 (3, 'Static', 2, NULL),
 (4, 'Echo', 2, 3),
 (5, 'Drift', NULL, 1),
 (6, 'Halcyon', 3, 3);
CREATE TABLE contracts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, runner_id INTEGER, target TEXT, payout INTEGER);
INSERT INTO contracts (id, runner_id, target, payout) VALUES
 (1, 1, 'Arasaka node', 5000),
 (2, 1, 'Militech vault', 8000),
 (3, 2, 'Biotechnica DB', 3000),
 (4, 3, 'Petrochem grid', 7000),
 (5, 6, 'NUSA relay', 4500);
exercises.starter: SELECT r.alias, m.alias FROM runners AS r JOIN runners AS m ON r.mentor_id = m.id ORDER BY r.alias;
exercises.solution: SELECT r.alias, m.alias, crews.name FROM runners AS r JOIN runners AS m ON r.mentor_id = m.id JOIN crews ON r.crew_id = crews.id ORDER BY r.alias;
exercises.hint: Keep the self-join (r, m), then add JOIN crews ON r.crew_id = crews.id for the student's crew name.
exercises.lore: Lineage and allegiance, traced in a single pull.
